meta.scenario: house same key same BPM — maximum compatibility, harmonic blend safe
meta.source: Palms Trax @ Boiler Room x Dekmantel 2018-08-04

transition_arc: Same key (12A/Dm) and same BPM — the most harmonically compatible pairing possible. With same-key blending, harmonic stems (other:0.6) can be brought in early without clashing. T1 from_bar=16 skips a 16-bar vocal intro that would clash with T2. This is a minimal-risk blend where the energy barely dips — T2 groove reinforces T1 groove.

annotated_actions.t1_state: T1 bar 16: first full groove bar after vocal intro, kick+bass active, RMS 0.48
annotated_actions.why: from_bar=16 skips T1's opening 16-bar vocal/spoken intro which would be incompatible with mixing.

annotated_actions.t1_state: T1 bar 72 (play bar 56 + from_bar 16 = track bar 72): drums=0.3, rms=0.30 — approaching outro
annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 0: groove intro, kick+bass from bar 0, RMS 0.40
annotated_actions.why: T1 at track bar 72 is winding toward outro. Same key means harmonic blend during the fade is safe throughout.

annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 0: kick present from bar 0 (drums=0.6), bass=0.0 for first 8 bars
annotated_actions.why: T2 from_bar=0: this track opens directly with its groove. Same key means other:0.6 harmonic blending is immediately safe. bass:0.0 still — even same-key, two basslines clash in the low end.

annotated_actions.t1_state: T1 play bar 64: drums=0.15, bass=0.20, rms=0.25 — fading
annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 8: bass now present (0.45), full groove imminent
annotated_actions.why: T2 bass has emerged at bar 8. Swap immediately — even same-key, two basslines at the same pitch phase-cancel in the low end.

annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 16: full groove, both kick and bassline locked in at 123 BPM, RMS 0.55
annotated_actions.why: T2 groove fully established. T1 completely faded.
